_comment: Claim-fidelity spec for the gather skill: does a real `/charness:gather` run honor its own central claims, above all that it routes to its references at the point of need? declaredReferences is the full reference set (coverage report). referenceEngagement classifies each reference on the engage-always/on-demand/gate-sufficient axis from the locked methodology contract (charness-artifacts/spec/2026-06-23-skill-claim-fidelity-doc-philosophy.md). requiredCommandFragments are the engage-always references SKILL.md routes to in a representative run: if the run never opens one, it did not follow its own routing. thresholds are intentionally omitted until a first baseline capture sets a per-skill runtime budget. requiredCommandFragments rationale: These four references encode the core decision axes of gather: which source to prioritize, where artifacts live, which provider mode is available, and which acquisition path is strongest — all needed to make the central judgment in a representative run.

adapter-contract.md.rationale: Bootstrap section requires resolving the adapter to determine artifact location and current-pointer mechanics before any acquisition begins.

asset-refresh.md.rationale: Provides the specific mechanics for safely writing dated records and refreshing the current pointer, only consulted when writing the result.
asset-refresh.md.trigger: When ready to write the durable asset to disk in step 4, ensuring symlink-aware pointer refresh and dated record handling.

browser-mediated-private-sources.md.rationale: Provides the decision ladder and artifact notes for browser-mediated fallback, a specific escalation path only taken when stronger routes are exhausted.
browser-mediated-private-sources.md.trigger: When the user provides a private SaaS URL and both official API/export and host-mediated paths are unavailable or insufficient.

capability-contract.md.rationale: Defines the access-mode decision ladder (grant/binary/env/public/human-only/degraded), browser-mediated fallback rules, and durable artifact shape that govern step 3's acquisition path selection.

document-seams.md.rationale: Suggests checking for existing repo-specific knowledge surfaces before defaulting to charness-artifacts/gather/; only relevant if the repo has established one.
document-seams.md.trigger: When the repo has a custom checked-in knowledge surface (docs folder, references directory, knowledge folder) that should override the default artifact location.

gather-provider.md.rationale: The planner and workflow must read this to determine which provider mode is available (direct-cli, host-mediated, or none) before attempting acquisition.

google-workspace-access.md.rationale: Directs operator to use the workspace path adviser script and defines the preference order (host-mediated > operator export > browser-mediated), only consulted for Google sources.
google-workspace-access.md.trigger: When the source is identified as Google Docs, Drive, Sheets, or Slides content requiring access beyond a public URL.

source-priority.md.rationale: The skill's step 1 explicitly routes to source priority rules to identify which source to gather from and prefer primary sources over derivatives.
